dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8569 IH: No Bias in the Baseline Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Cline introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Budget

form.official-title: To clarify that the baseline is based on current laws and the assumption of continuation of current levels of discretionary appropriations, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Bias in the Baseline Act.

subsection.text: Section 257 of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 (2 U.S.C. 907) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking subsection (a) and inserting the following:

subsection.text: For any budget year, the baseline refers to a projection, based on current laws and the assumption of continuation of current levels of discretionary appropriations, of current-year levels of new budget authority, outlays, revenues, and the surplus or deficit into the budget year and the outyears based on laws enacted through the applicable date.

subparagraph.text: by striking paragraph (1) and inserting the following:

paragraph.text: Laws providing or creating direct spending and receipts are assumed to operate in the manner specified in those laws for each such year.

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraph (3) as paragraph (2); and

in the second sentence of paragraph (1), by striking all that follows after current year, through the period and inserting excluding resources designated as an emergency requirement and any resources provided in supplemental appropriation laws.;
by redesignating paragraph (6) as paragraph (2); and
by inserting after paragraph (2) the following:

paragraph.text: No adjustment shall be made for inflation or for any other factor.

paragraph.header: Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974
paragraph.text: Section 202(e)(1) of the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 (2 U.S.C. 602(e)(1)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by inserting and before (B) the levels; and
by striking , and (C) and all that follows through 1985.

paragraph.text: Section 403(a)(3) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 603(a)(3)) is amended—

section.header: CBO publication of alternative fiscal scenarios
section.text: Section 202(e)(1) of the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 (2 U.S.C. 602(e)(1)) is amended by adding after the period at the end the following new sentence: Such report shall include alternative fiscal scenarios, as deemed appropriate, prepared in consultation with the Committees on the Budget of the House of Representatives and the Senate..
